role_summary: Survivor of the January 10, 1966 KKK firebombing that killed her husband Vernon Dahmer, Forrest County NAACP president; later elected Election Commissioner of District 2, Forrest County (1992).
biographical_paragraph: On the morning of January 10, 1966, Ku Klux Klansmen firebombed the Dahmer home in Hattiesburg, Mississippi; Ellie Dahmer pulled her children out of the burning house while her husband Vernon, then president of the Forrest County NAACP, held the attackers at bay with return fire as the rest of the family fled [src: 1]. Vernon died that day from smoke inhalation and damage to his lungs; the couple's daughter Bettie suffered serious burns to her arms [src: 1]. Ellie Jewel Davis Dahmer had been born June 27, 1925; she was a schoolteacher from Rose Hill, Mississippi when she and Vernon married in March 1952 [src: 1]. In 1992, twenty-six years after the attack, she was elected Election Commissioner of District 2 in Forrest County, the same district where her husband had been killed for his voter-registration work [src: 1]. Her Library of Congress oral history is entry 36 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1].

sources.title: Ellie Dahmer oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
